_comment: Shared cross-engine benchmark corpus for go-ruby-regexp (the from-scratch Onigmo reimplementation, formerly go-onigmo) vs Onigmo 6.2.0 (C) vs Go stdlib regexp (RE2). Each case is run identically by every harness. 'haystack' = 'unit' repeated 'repeat' times, then 'suffix' appended (suffix lets a literal land at the end of the input). Correctness is verified at runtime by cross-checking the leftmost-match byte span across our engine, the C Onigmo, and Ruby (and noting where RE2 leftmost-longest legitimately differs). 're2' marks whether Go's RE2 stdlib supports the construct at all.

cases.name: literal_miss
cases.unit: the quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog.

cases.name: literal_hit_end
cases.desc: literal search, hit at the very end
cases.unit: the quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog.

cases.name: email
cases.desc: realistic email pattern, hit near end
cases.unit: user logged in from host without contact details here today

cases.name: url
cases.desc: realistic URL pattern, hit near end
cases.unit: plain log line with no link in it at all here today ok

cases.name: ipv4

cases.unit: connection from client established and closed cleanly here

cases.name: logline
